desc: CONFIRMATION: GRPO step-3000 greedy, HF backend, rev 63870ec, A40 pod p44nx27xkitomr
note: DECISIVE: weights regenerate coherent ~44%-class math live under HF backend. Confirms 11% was an eval artifact, not collapse. Full-500 + vLLM bug-repro pending.

desc: CONFIRMATION full-500 greedy, HF backend, rev 63870ec, A40 pod p44nx27xkitomr, greedy500_confirm.py
note: BULLETPROOF: live full-500 reproduces archived 44.2% within 2 problems. POC confirmed end-to-end under pinned code.

desc: SFT final ckpt re-eval attempt on A40 (pod whd2pqc6spr736). Goal: trustworthy full-500 SFT c/n. RESULT: inference stack BROKEN, no number obtained.
model: heyalexchoi/qwen3-1.7b-math-sft (local sha 9822507c09f03b5474cec78cc118ee618cd3069c353f5144d8d0083adfb886af, verified identical on pod)
finding: vLLM 0.8.5 OVER-DEGENERATES vs real model: same sha-verified bytes + same sampler (temp0.6/top_p0.95/top_k20/rep1.05/8192tok) gave number_theory/572 = 0/8 (all peg 8192) where April-HF got 8/8 clean; 807 7/8->0/8; 2584 6/8->0/8. Format verified to match training (apply_chat_template prefix == training render). Conclusion: stack artifact, not weights. HF backend on this stack also broken (echoes problem / 'Assistant' repetition). Need pinned+canary'd stack (HF backend; gate on 572>=8/8). April HF log remains the trustworthy SFT evidence: easy-solve/hard-collapse, undertrained thinking model.
note: Code/env NOT pinned (recurring wound). vLLM has never reliably worked in this project. No trustworthy full-500 SFT number exists yet.

desc: REFINES the prior 2026-06-01 row: the vLLM stack is NOT globally broken.
finding: Stock official Qwen/Qwen3-1.7B on the SAME vLLM 0.8.5 stack solved 572 cleanly 4/4 (closes </think>, boxed{9} correct, 1483-2218 tok). So vLLM here is fine; the 0/8 collapse is SPECIFIC to our undertrained SFT checkpoint. Causes (not isolated): (a) genuinely fragile/marginal model sensitive to inference numerics, (b) reconstruction confound = extra_special_tokens list->{} patch for tf4.51 vs April tf5.5.3 intact. Neutralizing checkpoint generation_config.json (do_sample:false) did NOT fix it (sub-suspect ruled out). Implication: don't say 'vLLM broken'; our cold-start is fragile/stack-sensitive (8/8 HF-April vs 0/8 vLLM-now on same problem) = reinforces 'undertrained, needs better SFT data'.
note: Core finding (undertrained, intermittent collapse) rests on April-HF primary data, unaffected. No trustworthy full-500 exists; reproduce April HF+tf5.5.3 env + 572>=8/8 canary for a real number.

desc: TRUSTWORTHY full-500 SFT greedy MATH-500 — the long-missing number. Blessed stack: HF backend, transformers 5.5.3, torch 2.4.1+cu124, L40S community pod 67a0k029vw3tjm. Gated on 572 canary (8/8). --n_samples 0 greedy-only.

comparison.note: GRPO-from-base (no teacher traces) 43.8% > SFT-from-distillation 40.2%, AND GRPO is concise (~80 tok median, ~0% peg) vs SFT 53% peg. SFT accuracy is termination-gated. Both greedy/HF/math-verify; budgets differ (SFT 8192 thinking vs GRPO ~2k non-thinking) but GRPO's budget never binds. Different output regimes (thinking vs non-thinking) from same base.

finding: SFT verbose-distillation is functional but termination-crippled; concise distillation is the lever (see docs/distill-trace-framework.md). vLLM ruled out as usable for this ckpt (see eval_results/vllm_canary_2026-06-07/FINDINGS.md).

config: configs/sft_config_v2.yaml (3 epochs, seq 2560, eff batch 16, lr 2e-5)

eval.termination: 500/500 finish=stop, zero 8192 pegs; canary 19/25 max 453 tok

verdict: concise-distillation thesis CONFIRMED: +9.2pt over v1, +5.2pt over GRPO POC, termination disease cured

note: greedy measured twice: 49.4% (greedy-only run) and 50.6% (regenerated during pass@8 run) — 6-problem delta is vLLM continuous-batching nondeterminism; canonical record = final mv_rescored file (50.6). Comparisons: base 35.8/65.0/24.55, GRPO-3000 44.2/71.4/36.83.
